channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Put lids on circular bins on Haggerston road, 20th May
item.description:
The round bins here and nearby have no lids, and so rubbish often ends up blowing around and out of the bins due to wind. This ends up with the road having a lot of rubbish on it every day. This would be solved by the bins having lids.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Haggerston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Yellow lines non existent, 20th May
item.description:
The yellow lines in crescent drive (between Lakeswood road and Chesham Ave) has completely disappeared as you can see in the photos provided. We have a no parking sign between 8-10am but it’s a bit pointless as there are no yellow lines so cars park there at all times. Please repaint asap as this is the 4th time in 3 years that it’s been reported.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crescent Drive, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Gedling House East Dulwich estate, 20th May
item.description:
We found the house hold near the bin dump in the corner main enterance of Gedling house. East Dulwich estate.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pytchley Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Severe health hazard from bins, 20th May
item.description:
90 Whiston Road.Our bins have not been collected in 5+ weeks. Some bags were removed from the floor but the contents of the large bins remain full. The room, surrounding building, and inside the lobby have become infested with flies and rats at the door.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Whiston Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Green bin left out on pavement blocking it since 13/05/2026, 20th May
item.description:
Green bin has been left out on footpath for over a week blocking it at the narrowest point. A wheelchair user would have to go onto the road to get around it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Holbeach Gardens, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox in the courtyard, 20th May
item.description:
We have a dead fox in the courtyard (Jubilee yard, in front of the entrance to the pyramid building in the courtyard) since sunday. I reported it already, but no one has removed it. Please send someone to remove it.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 31, Queen Elizabeth Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The LED street lamp opposite 64 Bruce Close is not working., 20th May
item.description:
The street light opposite 64 Bruce Close is not working. It is the only source of light at the end of Bruce Close, which is surrounded by shrubs and bushes that can hide people from view. The lack of lighting is a concern for local residents. Please address as soon as possible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bruce Close, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti problem, 20th May
item.description:
There are several areas of graffiti around carters Croft which are unsightly and intimidating for my tenants and residents. Could they be removed please? Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Red Post Hill, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven road full of weed, 20th May
item.description:
This path connects Winn rd with Burnt Ash, workers came a while back to fix some pipes but when they closed it they did not do a great job. First they left building material rubbish/dust then the closed a line of that very small road making it even more uneven and now all the weeds is growing too much to the side. My mum an old women has tripped twice there. Please send someone to clear all debris and pave it properly from side to side

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Winn Road, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish, flytip under over grown trees, 20th May
item.description:
This area gets a lot of fly tip and rubbish thrown there as the tree trunks has grown big branch’s that hides a lot of rubbish. Please send someone to clear the rubbish and fly tip and also cut down the branches and the trees. It is to the top of Winn Rd.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 20, Winn Road, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping on Frederick place corner by Burrage Road, 20th May
item.description:
Usual fly tipping spot; photo taken 20th May 2026

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Burrage Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bee or wasp nest on edge of pond in flower garden of Kennington park, 20th May
item.description:
Many bees or wasps swarming around pond so visitors to flower garden likely to get stung.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elis Terrace, Oval, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bucket and mop by the tree stump.at the Lomdon Road junction of Oaklands Road, 20th May
item.description:
This was reported on the 19th May. I received an email, reporting it as fixed. However, it was not removed. Purely the gas cannister that was sat in it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oaklands Road, Shortlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned/stripped bicycle, 20th May
item.description:
There is a stripped and abandoned bicycle left on Arnold Estate, near the Tanner Street entrance into the Estate.It appears to have been stripped and is no longer usable. Please can it be removed.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 142-179, Sweeney Crescent, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black rubbish bag, 20th May
item.description:
Rubbish bag left next to the gate of 496 Lordship Lane and next to the BT Exchange. Several weeks. Can it be removed please? Thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 496, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytip of mattress and other, 20th May
item.description:
Flytip of mattress and other waste

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Ravenscourt Road, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping - black rubbish bag, 20th May
item.description:
Black rubbish bag next to the gate of 496 Lordship Lane on the BT exchange side. Kindly remove it. Thank you.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 496, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping onto railway sidings, 20th May
item.description:
Sunday 17th at 2.45pm I saw my neighbour chuck a load of rubbish over her garden back fence into the bushes at the side of the railway. There was a lot of it, including large items. I ran inside to get my phone & videoed the last thing she threw, but I’ve not been able to upload a video here. The address is 240 Amherst Drive.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 240, Amherst Drive, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress dumped by alley next to number 9, 20th May
item.description:
Old mattress dumped.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fendyke Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The wall seen in the picture belongs to Highways located just above M25 Orbital which is well mantained on the oposite side. Residents would appreciate if the bushes and path on the residents side of the wall can be cleared as the current state is very unplesant and if prolongs may attrackt pests. We thank your teams in advance, 20th May
item.description:
Bushes, weed, lettering on other side of highway managed walls on top of M25

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Holmesdale Tunnel, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
